items.headline: Mixed Sentiment Dominates as Investors Weigh Inflation, Geopolitics, and Earnings
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a mixed trading session today as cooler-than-expected inflation data provided some relief, while ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated and dampened broader sentiment. Asian markets closed mixed, with some tech-heavy indexes experiencing a sell-off, and European markets edged lower. Investors are keenly watching upcoming retail sales figures and new jobless claims for further economic direction.

items.headline: PayPal Surges on Takeover Bid Report
items.summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. ($PYPL) jumped 17.21% following reports of a potential $53 billion takeover bid from Stripe and Advent.

items.headline: China Pharma Sees Massive Gains
items.summary: China Pharma ($CPHI) was among the day's biggest gainers, soaring 78.9% amid a relatively calm broader market.

items.headline: BlackRock Boosted by Q2 Earnings
items.summary: BlackRock Inc. ($BLK) gained 6.63% after reporting strong Q2 earnings and receiving higher analyst price targets, including an upgrade to Overweight from JPMorgan.

items.headline: Triller Plunges in Volatile Session
items.summary: Triller ($ILLR) saw a significant decline today, falling 44.6% in an extreme single-stock move.

items.headline: IBM Drops After Missed Preliminary Earnings
items.summary: IBM Corp. ($IBM) fell approximately 25% after preliminary revenue and adjusted earnings missed expectations, with full results awaited next week.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Declines
items.summary: Dell Technologies Inc. ($DELL) closed down 9.80% today.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Released Today and Ahead
items.summary: June U.S. consumer prices fell 0.4%, with core inflation flat at 2.6% over the past year, easing some inflation concerns. Today saw the release of crucial June retail sales data, weekly initial jobless claims, and the Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index for July. Speeches by FOMC members Lorie Logan and Jeffrey Schmid are also on the schedule.

items.headline: Major Companies Report Earnings
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc. ($UNH) reported strong Q2 2026 results, with revenues of $112.0 billion and adjusted EPS of $6.38, and raised its full-year outlook. TSMC ($TSM) shares were down 1.4% in after-hours trading after its results failed to inspire optimism, despite record preliminary Q2 revenue. Netflix ($NFLX) and GE Aerospace ($GE) are also set to report earnings today. Citizens Financial Group Inc. ($CFG) reported a 35% year-over-year increase in Q2 net income and a 41% rise in EPS.

items.headline: Technology and Financials Lead as Market Shifts from Defensive Stance
items.summary: The Information Technology and Financials sectors are showing leadership, driven by expectations for semiconductor demand, lower interest rates, and strong corporate earnings. The market appears to be shifting from a defensive to a more offensive stance, favoring growth stories like AI and semiconductors over geopolitical risks. However, US stock futures for tech stocks turned negative, and the Nasdaq slipped as tech stocks lost momentum. Meanwhile, defensive sectors such as utilities and consumer staples have lagged.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Fuel Oil Price Concerns
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran remain a principal source of uncertainty, continuing to underpin crude oil prices. Brent crude was down 1.67% at $84.16 a barrel, while WTI crude fell to $79.34. The conflict contributes to concerns over inflation and potential disruptions to global energy shipping routes.
